subject: reading
title: Grade 3 ELA — Released Practice
instructions: Released-form sample. Choose the best answer for each question.

questions.questionText: Based on the passage, why was Tony interested in marionettes?
options: He liked to watch big parades.
He wanted to take care of animals.
He was curious about how things moved.
He was interested in writing puppet shows.
questions.explanation: Tony was curious about how things moved, which is why he found marionettes interesting. They are puppets that move in a special way, and that made him want to learn more!

questions.questionText: Based on paragraphs 2–4, what does the diagram mainly show?
options: how the invention was like a puppet
why feeding chickens was a hard chore
how the invention was supposed to work
why feeding chickens was done in the morning
questions.explanation: The diagram mainly shows how the invention was supposed to work, helping us understand its purpose and how it functions. This is important because it explains the idea behind the invention clearly.

questions.questionText:
Read the sentence from paragraph 5 in the box below.

Word soon spread about Tony’s amazing marionettes.

What does the sentence suggest about Tony’s marionettes?
options: They were changed over the years.
They were copied by other people.
questions.explanation: The sentence suggests that Tony’s marionettes became very popular because word spread about them, meaning many people wanted to see and talk about them!

questions.questionText: Based on the passage, why did Macy’s decide to use puppets instead of live animals in the parade?
options: Live animals cost too much to use.
Live animals became too tired to walk.
questions.explanation: Macy’s decided to use puppets instead of live animals because some people were scared of the animals, making the parade more fun and safe for everyone.

questions.questionText: Based on paragraph 21, why did Tony think the puppets needed to be bigger and fly higher?
options: so that the puppets would look more real
so that people could see the puppets better
so that people could stand under the puppets
so that the puppets would look more beautiful
questions.explanation: Tony thought the puppets needed to be bigger and fly higher so that people could see them better from far away. This way, everyone could enjoy the show!

questions.questionText: What does the question in paragraph 23 mainly show?
questions.explanation: The question in paragraph 23 mainly shows what Tony wondered about, which means he was thinking and curious about something important to him.

questions.questionText: In paragraph 28, how did Tony most likely feel?
questions.explanation: In paragraph 28, Tony most likely felt nervous because he was probably anxious about how the parade would go and if everything would be okay.

questions.questionText:
Read the sentence from paragraph 30 in the box below.

Then, one by one, Tony cut the lines to the sandbags . . .

Based on the passage, what did Tony’s action show?
questions.explanation: When Tony cut the lines to the sandbags, it showed that the parade was beginning because that action was part of getting everything ready for the big event!

questions.questionText: In paragraph 7, what does “danced across the stage as if by magic” suggest about the marionettes?
questions.explanation: The phrase 'danced across the stage as if by magic' suggests that the marionettes seemed alive because they moved in a way that looked magical and lively, capturing everyone’s attention!

questions.questionText:
Read paragraph 12 in the box below.

In fact, Macy’s first parade was such a success that they decided to have one every year on Thanksgiving Day—to celebrate America’s own holiday.

In the paragraph, which of the following words is a noun?
questions.explanation: In the paragraph, the word 'holiday' is a noun because it names a special day, while the other words are not nouns. Nouns are names of people, places, or things, and 'holiday' fits that description perfectly!

questions.questionText: Based on paragraph 2 of the article, why do penguins “shoot up out of the water”?
questions.explanation: Penguins shoot up out of the water to breathe because they need air after swimming underwater. Just like we come up for air when we swim, penguins do the same!

questions.questionText: Based on the article, which would be the best heading for paragraphs 1–3?
options: A Meal Fit for a King
questions.explanation: The best heading for paragraphs 1–3 is 'On the Move' because those paragraphs talk about how penguins travel and move around, especially when they are swimming or walking.

questions.questionText: Based on the article, which of the following happens last?
options: The female penguin lays an egg.
The male penguin goes to the ocean to eat.
The male penguin protects the egg with his feet.
The female penguin comes back from the ocean.
questions.explanation: The last thing that happens is the male penguin goes to the ocean to eat. This happens after the female lays the egg and after he has been protecting it, so it comes last in the order of events.

questions.questionText: Based on the article and the poem, why do male penguins put their eggs and chicks on their feet?
questions.explanation: Male penguins put their eggs and chicks on their feet because the ice is very cold. This helps keep the eggs and chicks warm and safe from the freezing temperatures.

questions.questionText:
Read the sentence from paragraph 1 of the article in the box below.

"They plunge into the icy ocean to catch their meals of fish, squid, and krill."

Based on the article, which word could be used instead of plunge?
questions.explanation: The word 'dive' can be used instead of 'plunge' because both words mean to jump into the water quickly. When penguins dive, they go underwater to catch their food.
